title: Cost & scaling (autoscaling, spot, distillation) + DSA 2-D DP
focus: Unit-cost-per-prediction; KEDA scaling; spot training without losing work.

items.label: Cost modeling: training vs inference, batching, autoscaling
interviewQuestions: How would you model the unit cost of a prediction in production?
What levers reduce inference cost (batching, quantization, caching, distillation)?

items.label: Autoscaling: HPA, KEDA, queue-based
interviewQuestions: Compare CPU-based HPA vs queue-based KEDA scaling for ML inference.
Why does GPU-pinned inference often defeat HPA, and how do you actually scale GPU pods?

items.label: Spot/preemptible instances for training
interviewQuestions: How would you train safely on spot instances (checkpointing, retries)?
When does spot training become NET more expensive than on-demand — what's the breakeven?

tracks.label: Production ML · FinOps and cloud trade-offs

items.label: Unit economics: cost per prediction, training run, eval suite, tenant, and feature
interviewQuestions: How do you calculate cost per prediction for a model behind online inference?
How do you attribute GPU, vector DB, LLM API, and storage costs to a product feature?
What cost regression should fail a release even if quality improves slightly?

items.label: Managed ML services vs custom infra: SageMaker, Vertex AI, Kubernetes, and hosted APIs
interviewQuestions: When do you choose SageMaker or Vertex AI over a custom Kubernetes serving stack?
What operational burden do you accept when you build the platform yourself?
Which requirements force you away from a hosted API or managed service?

references.label: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer exam guide
Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer exam guide
